title: Odyssey, Book 1: The Counsel of the Gods
description: Book 1 of Homer's Odyssey: The Counsel of the Gods.
introduction: Book 1 of the Odyssey: The Counsel of the Gods. The Greek text follows the standard Homeric vulgate.

translation.original_text: Ἄνδρα μοι ἔννεπε, Μοῦσα, πολύτροπον, ὃς μάλα πολλὰ πλάγχθη, ἐπεὶ Τροίης ἱερὸν πτολίεθρον ἔπερσε.
translation.text: Tell me, O Muse, of the man of many turns, who wandered far and wide after he sacked the sacred city of Troy. Many were the peoples whose cities he saw and whose minds he learned, and many the sorrows he suffered at sea. On Olympus, Athena pleaded with Zeus to let Odysseus return home. She descended to Ithaca disguised as Mentes and told Telemachus to seek news of his father.
